name: CreateMandate
description: Create a new mandate for a customer to authorize recurring payments. IMPORTANT: In most cases, mandates are created automatically when a customer makes their first payment. Manual mandate creation is only needed for specific use cases like importing existing mandates or creating SEPA Direct Debit mandates with pre-existing authorization. Requires the customer's name and payment method details (IBAN for direct debit, card token for credit card, email for PayPal). This endpoint requires the 'mandates.write' scope.

customer_id.description: The ID of the customer to create a mandate for (e.g., 'cst_abc123').

method.description: The payment method for the mandate. Supported values: 'directdebit' (SEPA Direct Debit), 'creditcard', 'paypal'. The method determines which additional fields are required.

consumer_name.description: The full name of the consumer (account holder) who is giving the mandate authorization. Required for all payment methods.

consumer_account.description: For 'directdebit': The consumer's IBAN (e.g., 'NL55INGB0000000000'). For 'creditcard': The card token from a previous payment. Leave empty for 'paypal'.

consumer_bic.description: For 'directdebit': Optional BIC/SWIFT code of the consumer's bank (e.g., 'INGBNL2A'). Not used for other payment methods.

consumer_email.description: For 'paypal': The consumer's email address. Not used for other payment methods.

signature_date.description: Optional date when the mandate was signed by the consumer in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2025-01-15'). Defaults to today if not provided.

mandate_reference.description: Optional custom reference for the mandate. If not provided, Mollie generates a unique reference automatically.

paypal_billing_agreement_id.description: For 'paypal': The billing agreement ID from PayPal (e.g., 'B-12345').

pay_pal_vault_id.description: For 'paypal': The Vault ID from PayPal (e.g., '8kk8451t').

testmode.description: Enable test mode. Set to 'true' to create a test mode mandate, or omit for live mode.
